# SIML - Selectable Image Markup Language

> SIML makes the text inside raster images (PNG/JPEG/WebP) selectable, copyable,
> and machine-readable: a PDF-style text layer bound to the image at three
> independent tiers so it survives real-world distribution - re-encoding,
> messaging apps like WhatsApp, and screenshots. Recovered values are always
> integrity-checked: SIML returns the exact authored text or nothing, never a
> wrong answer. The spec, reader, and reference encoder are CC0 (public domain).

## How it works

- T0 Container: the full text layer (objects, positions, types, styles) as JSON
  in a JUMBF box (the same ISO container family C2PA uses). Lossless; dies on
  re-encode.
- T1 Watermark: a short typed field (e.g. a phone number) embedded in the
  pixels via a dual-band DCT-QIM watermark with Reed-Solomon + CRC. Survives
  JPEG recompression down to q30 offline; smooth image regions stay visually
  untouched.
- T1 verify mode (draft v0.4): the watermark carries a checksum of the visible
  text; OCR plus a checksum-guided search recovers the exact text and its true
  glyph positions.
- T2 Fingerprint: a 256-bit perceptual hash resolves the full layer from a
  registry, surviving aggressive downscales and screenshots, with mandatory
  near-duplicate pixel verification before anything is surfaced.

## Try it

- /create - editor: place text on an image, export with the tiers you choose
- /view - viewer: drop any image, watch the recovery pipeline run, simulate a
  platform strip-and-recompress

## Source and specification

- Repository (CC0): https://github.com/lumos021/siml
- Current spec: https://github.com/lumos021/siml/blob/main/SIML-SPEC-v0.3.md
- Verified visible text draft: https://github.com/lumos021/siml/blob/main/SIML-SPEC-v0.4-draft.md
- Integration guide: https://github.com/lumos021/siml/blob/main/INTEGRATION.md
- Roadmap: https://github.com/lumos021/siml/blob/main/ROADMAP.md
- npm packages: siml-writer (Node embed), siml-reader (browser read)
